chief_complaint: A 51-year-old female with a history of Lynch syndrome and metastatic urothelial carcinoma treated with pembrolizumab presented with a pancreatic mass identified during follow-up by PET/CT.
history: The patient was treated with pembrolizumab for metastatic urothelial carcinoma. A pancreatic mass was identified during follow-up by PET/CT. Pathology from endoscopic ultrasound-guided fine-needle aspiration (EUS-FNA) could not exclude adenocarcinoma. Surgical resection revealed pathology consistent with type II autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: PET/CT scan: pancreatic mass identified. EUS-FNA: pathology consistent with adenocarcinoma (could not exclude). Surgical pathology: type II autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP).
